response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, there are critical misalignments with mandatory requirements. The resume shows only ~6 months of C++ experience from a decade ago with no recent hands-on work, no Windows platform focus, no SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and crucially, zero financial services or capital markets domain experience. This specialized mid-senior role requires recent, deep C++ expertise paired with capital markets knowledge—neither of which the candidate possesses.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, architecture design) across multiple complex systems
Proven ability to learn diverse technologies and lead technically across web, embedded, and backend domains
Clear communication skills evidenced by workshop delivery and mentoring experience
concerns: C++ experience limited to ~6 months (2014-2015) with no recent work; 10-year gap makes skills likely severely outdated for capital markets requirements
No exposure to financial services, fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domains—critical for understanding system architecture and requirements
No explicit Windows platform development experience and 10-year career trajectory focused on cross-platform/Linux, web technologies, and data platforms

key_factors.factor: Zero domain expertise in capital markets/fixed income
C++ experience outdated and insufficient (6 months vs. 5-10 years required)
Strong general software engineering fundamentals and proven communication
